course: CMU 21-254: Linear Algebra and Vector Calculus for Engineers
audience: SI leader teaching live on a whiteboard. This is not a student worksheet.
toneRule: Keep explanations very simple, verbal, and board-friendly. Avoid textbook phrasing unless the problem needs it.
title: Line Integrals, Conservative Fields, and Path Independence
sessionGoal: Students should be able to compute scalar and vector line integrals and use potential functions when a field is conservative.
quickStartIfUnprepared: Start with the difference: scalar line integral uses ds; vector line integral uses dr.
Do Problem 1 for scalar line integrals.
Do Problem 2 for direct vector line integral setup.
Do Problem 3 for conservative fields and path independence.
sayThisFirst: A scalar line integral adds up a scalar quantity along a curve.
A vector line integral measures how much a vector field pushes along the path.
For \(\int_C F\cdot dr\), plug in the curve and dot with \(r'(t)\).
If \(F=\nabla f\), then the line integral only depends on endpoints.
boardPlan: Write the two line-integral templates side by side.
Do one scalar line integral using \(ds=\|r'(t)\|dt\).
Do one vector line integral using \(F(r(t))\cdot r'(t)\).
Finish with a potential function and endpoint shortcut.
keyFormulas: Vector line integral: \(\int_C F\cdot dr=\int_a^b F(r(t))\cdot r'(t)\,dt\).
For \(F=\langle P,Q\rangle\), conservative usually means \(P_y=Q_x\) on a nice region.
commonMistakes: Using \(ds\) in a vector line integral.
Forgetting to plug the curve into the field.
Using the endpoint shortcut before checking the field is conservative.
Subtracting endpoints in the wrong order.
notationWarnings: Use \(dr\) or \(F\cdot dr\) for vector line integrals.
For conservative fields, clearly name the potential function, often \(\phi\) or \(f\).

problems.title: Scalar line integral along a line segment
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(C\) be the line segment from \((0,0)\) to \((3,4)\). Compute \(\int_C (x+y)\,ds\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: because this has \(ds\), we need the speed factor \(\|r'(t)\|\).

drawInstructions: Shade/mark that x+y is sampled along the segment.

commonMistakes: Using \(r'(t)\) as a vector instead of its length.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 8 for line integrals.

problems.title: Vector line integral by direct parametrization
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(F(x,y)=\langle y,x\rangle\) and let \(C\) be \(r(t)=(t,t^2)\), \(0\le t\le1\). Compute \(\int_C F\cdot dr\) directly.
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: for \(F\cdot dr\), the recipe is field along the curve dotted with velocity.

drawInstructions: Draw the parabola y=x^2 from (0,0) to (1,1).
Put arrows on the curve from left to right.
Draw a few field arrows F=<y,x> near the curve.
Write F(r(t)) dot r'(t) beside the picture.

walkthrough.label: Plug the curve into the field
walkthrough.latex: Along the curve, \(x=t\) and \(y=t^2\), so \[F(r(t))=\langle t^2,t\rangle.\]

commonMistakes: Forgetting to plug \(x=t\), \(y=t^2\) into the field.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 8 for vector line integrals.

problems.title: Conservative field and endpoint shortcut
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(F(x,y)=\langle 2xy+3,x^2+4y\rangle\). Show that \(F\) is conservative, find a potential function \(\phi\), and compute \(\int_C F\cdot dr\) from \((0,1)\) to \((2,-1)\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: if the field is conservative, the path itself does not matter. Only the start and end points matter.

drawInstructions: Draw two different paths from (0,1) to (2,-1).
Label both paths with the same endpoint value difference.

walkthrough.label: Check the conservative condition
walkthrough.latex: Here \(P=2xy+3\) and \(Q=x^2+4y\). \[P_y=2x,\qquad Q_x=2x.\] Since \(P_y=Q_x\), the field is conservative on \(\mathbb{R}^2\).

commonMistakes: Forgetting the \(g(y)\) term after integrating with respect to \(x\).
Using start minus end instead of end minus start.
Using the shortcut without first checking the field is conservative.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 8 for conservative fields.

wrapUpQuestions: What changes when a field is conservative?
qualityControlNotes: Checked conservative potential phi=x^2y+3x+2y^2 and endpoint integral value 2.
Visual specs distinguish scalar ds from vector dr.
reviewSummary: Session 19 is ready with checked line integral and conservative-field problems.
